subject: Polity
text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides the Right to Life and Personal Liberty?
explanation: Article 21 states: "No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law." It is the most expansively interpreted article in the Constitution.

subject: Polity
text: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment (1992) relates to which of the following?
explanation: The 73rd Amendment gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions, mandating a three-tier system (Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Samiti, Zila Parishad) in states with a population over 20 lakh.

subject: Geography
text: The Tropic of Cancer does NOT pass through which of the following Indian states?
explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 Indian states: Gujarat, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Tripura, and Mizoram. Maharashtra is NOT among them.

subject: Geography
text: Westerlies are planetary winds that blow from:
options: Equatorial low pressure to subtropical high pressure
Subtropical high pressure to sub-polar low pressure
Polar high pressure to sub-polar low pressure
Sub-polar low pressure to subtropical high pressure
explanation: Westerlies blow from subtropical high pressure belts (around 30° N/S) towards sub-polar low pressure belts (around 60° N/S). They are responsible for carrying weather systems in the mid-latitudes.

subject: Economy
text: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) was established as a statutory body in which year?
explanation: SEBI was set up as a non-statutory body in 1988, but received statutory status through the SEBI Act, 1992. It regulates and develops the securities market in India.

subject: History
text: Who was the first Governor-General of independent India?
explanation: Lord Mountbatten served as the first Governor-General of independent India from August 15, 1947 to June 21, 1948. He was succeeded by C. Rajagopalachari, the first and only Indian Governor-General.

subject: Economy
text: Which Five Year Plan introduced the concept of "Growth with Social Justice and Removal of Poverty"?
explanation: The 5th Five Year Plan (1974–79) introduced the twin objectives of "Garibi Hatao" (Remove Poverty) and "Growth with Social Justice." It also introduced the Minimum Needs Programme.

subject: History
text: The Treaty of Yandabo (1826) ended which conflict?
explanation: The Treaty of Yandabo was signed on February 24, 1826, between the British East India Company and Burma (Myanmar), ending the First Anglo-Burmese War. Under this treaty, Assam came under British control.

subject: Environment
text: Which of the following is a potent greenhouse gas with a global warming potential ~25 times that of CO₂ over 100 years?
explanation: Methane (CH₄) has a Global Warming Potential of about 25 over a 100-year period, making it much more potent than CO₂. It is released from livestock, rice paddies, landfills, and fossil fuel operations.

subject: Science & Tech
text: Which Indian space mission successfully demonstrated cryogenic engine technology indigenously for the first time?
explanation: GSLV-D5 (January 5, 2014) was the first successful flight of India's indigenous cryogenic upper stage (CE-7.5), making India the 6th country to master cryogenic rocket technology.
